name: Jet_Mech

infobox.Systems: Aerial Bombs(Brute)EffectFly over a target, dropping an explosive smoke bomb.(1 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases damage by 1.Upgrade II+1 Range ()Allows jumping over and attacking an additional target.
infobox.Special: Flying: Flying units can move over any terrain tile.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theRusting Hulkssquad.
sections.Strategy:
Remember thatSmokecancels enemy attacks, so even if the Jet Mech can't kill a Vek, it can stop it from doing any damage. In the Rusting Hulks squad, keep in mind thatElectric Smokewill also passively damage and eventually kill worn down enemy Vek.
Due to the leaping nature of the Jet Mech's attack requiring an empty tile to land on, it is easy to get stuck in Smoke itself. Therefore,Camilla Veracan be an excellent choice for a starting pilot, she also helps it keep it free from webbing enemies which can otherwise compromise its positioning. Another good choice isHarold Schmidtwho can provide push with his ability, which the Jet Mech uses well with its high flight mobility.
Utilizing the Jet Mech’s ability to launch itself over Vek can be invaluable for both damaging enemies and improving its position for subsequent turns, particularly with+1 Rangeupgraded. By attacking from different angles and at different distances, it can quickly gain the advantage using the smoke to cancel enemy attacks and restrict their movement, as enemies will avoid moving into Smoke if they have other options.
The Jet Mech does well staying close to VekSpawnsthanks to its direct attack utility and high starting movement, but it can also be quite frail at 2 HP to start. Consider increasing it as soon as it is feasible, or equipping a pilot with an HP+2 ability (or similar), as this will also allow it to land on spawn points more freely after it attacks to get more value out of its actions.
Under a Custom Squad, the Jet Mech can be used alongside theSmog Mechas well; the smoke it leaves behind can be used withNanofilter Mendingto repair it and other Mechs, including repairing disabled mechs to revive them.

Strategy
[
]
Remember that
Smoke
cancels enemy attacks, so even if the Jet Mech can't kill a Vek, it can stop it from doing any damage. In the Rusting Hulks squad, keep in mind that
Electric Smoke
will also passively damage and eventually kill worn down enemy Vek.
Due to the leaping nature of the Jet Mech's attack requiring an empty tile to land on, it is easy to get stuck in Smoke itself. Therefore,
Camilla Vera
can be an excellent choice for a starting pilot, she also helps it keep it free from webbing enemies which can otherwise compromise its positioning. Another good choice is
Harold Schmidt
who can provide push with his ability, which the Jet Mech uses well with its high flight mobility.
Utilizing the Jet Mech’s ability to launch itself over Vek can be invaluable for both damaging enemies and improving its position for subsequent turns, particularly with
+1 Range
upgraded. By attacking from different angles and at different distances, it can quickly gain the advantage using the smoke to cancel enemy attacks and restrict their movement, as enemies will avoid moving into Smoke if they have other options.
The Jet Mech does well staying close to Vek
Spawns
thanks to its direct attack utility and high starting movement, but it can also be quite frail at 2 HP to start. Consider increasing it as soon as it is feasible, or equipping a pilot with an HP+2 ability (or similar), as this will also allow it to land on spawn points more freely after it attacks to get more value out of its actions.
Under a Custom Squad, the Jet Mech can be used alongside the
Smog Mech
as well; the smoke it leaves behind can be used with
Nanofilter Mending
to repair it and other Mechs, including repairing disabled mechs to revive them.
